description: Bidirectional match rules for cross-language comparison. Each rule defines a C pattern and a Rust pattern that should be considered equivalent. When the comparator encounters these patterns on opposite sides, it matches their captured variables recursively.

match_rules.name: c_null_check_is_rust_is_none
match_rules.justification: C !handler → Rust handler.is_none(). Same null/None check.

match_rules.name: c_handler_exists_is_rust_is_some
match_rules.justification: C handler (truthy check) → Rust handler.is_some(). Same existence check.

match_rules.name: c_return_1_is_rust_return_void
match_rules.justification: C returns 1 for success. Rust returns void (implicit Ok).

match_rules.name: c_return_0_is_rust_return_error
match_rules.justification: C returns 0 for failure (OOM). Rust panics on OOM — no error return.

match_rules.name: c_handler_dispatch_is_rust_if_let
match_rules.justification: C: if (handler) { handler(args) }. Rust: if let Some(h) = &mut handler { h(args) }. Handler check + dispatch pattern.
